title: celtis punctata
intro: Celtis punctata, also known as the Mexican hackberry or sugar hackberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Celtis punctata include the potential treatment of various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, as well as its use as an anti-diarrheal agent. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and its bark, leaves, and roots are commonly used in herbal preparations.
